content.full_text:
June 17, 1976


J. Anthony Kline
Legal Affairs Secretary
Governor's Office
Capitol Mall
Sacramento CA 95814

Dear Tony:

     Peter Sly's question concerning gifts and contributions made by Marc Poche to the Governor has been referred to me for a response. As I understand the question, it is whether the prohibition in Government Code Section 86300 against gifts by employees of the State of California who attempt to influence legislative action and would be lobbying but for the exemption provided by that Section applies to gifts and contributions by Mr. Poche to the Governor.

     Since the general prohibitions against lobbyists expressly refer to both gifts and contributions (§§86202, 86203), it can be presumed that the failure to include "contributions" in Section 86300 was deliberate. Contributions, therefore, are not prohibited.

     The question with respect to gifts is more complex because the section does not specifically exclude gifts by a state employee to his own superior. The staff has concluded, however, that no legitimate purpose would be served by extending the prohibition against gifts in Section 86300 to gifts by Mr. Poche to the Governor. The prohibition was intended to prevent state employees who otherwise would be lobbyists from making gifts to state candidates, elected state officials, legislative officials or agency officials for the purpose of improperly influencing the decisions of those individuals. In the case of gifts by Mr. Poche, who is a personal aid to the Governor and acts as his agent, the purpose of the prohibition is not frustrated by excluding him from its coverage.

     I should caution you that this advice is limited to instances involving gifts from the Governor's personal staff and does¿include all gifts from employees in the executive branch. Furthermore, although the staff believes that this advice comports with the purposes of the Political Reform Act, the provision in question is not clear and is subject to different interpretations. The advice provided herein precludes the Commission from initiating an enforcement proceeding based on actions taken pursuant to the advice, but does not prevent a person or other agency from instituting a court proceeding.

OVER ALL SHORT

CALIFORNIA IS
[illegible] AT
GUIDED PRODUCES
ITEM DESIGNED
RAISE TO [illegible]

ABOUT FROM


J. Anthony Kline
Legal Affairs Secretary
June 17, 1976
Page Two


     I hope the foregoing is helpful. If you have any further
questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

                                   Very truly yours,


                                   Kenneth Finney
                                   Chief
                                   Legal Division

KF:pvp

BRITISH MUSEUM, W.C.
[illegible]
[illegible] WILL SOON
OWN [illegible]

[illegible] we owed nor it [illegible] xi [illegible] and [illegible]
I am [illegible] to [illegible] [illegible] you so candle [illegible]

[illegible] vitriol very

Vendit [illegible]
